data.name: AfterTaskSelection_Help
data.value: If you choose something other than 'do nothing' program will configure OS to show you report on next your logon to the system if any errors or warnings will be registered.

data.name: BackupVersion_Delete_Confirm
data.value:
Do you really want to delete version "{0}"?
Deleted version changes will become part of version "{1}".

data.name: BackupVersion_Viewer_Help
data.value: Select file or folder you want to restore in tree and click "Restore".

data.name: BUtilServer_Error_ConnectionAborted
data.value: Connection to server was unexpectedly terminated (Are passwords on Client and Server are the same?)

data.name: CommandLineArguments_Help
data.value: Visit {0} to see command line arguments.

data.name: DataStorage_Field_UploadQuota_Help
data.value: Limits amount of data being sent during one task execution to storage. 0 - no limit. When quota is exceeded, other files will be skipped and will be processed next time. Usage scenario: You copy data to remote server. There's a problem: ISP drops upload speed after several gigabytes. This setting allows you to handle it.

data.name: DataStorage_Field_UploadQuota_Validation
data.value: Quota should be 0 (do not apply) or value above 0.

data.name: DataStorage_Script_Help
data.value: If folder becomes available after executing some commands, specify {0} scripts for connection and disconnection.

data.name: FtpsServerTask_Help
data.value: You can share any folder for read-write access via FTPS protocol (explicit encryption with self-signed certificate) inside your local area network.

data.name: Icons_Help_Link
data.value: Add character icon to your text

data.name: ImportMediaTask_Field_TransformFileName_Example
data.value: Example: Imagine you have file {0} (modified at {1}). With this directory and template string file will be stored as: {2}

data.name: ImportMediaTask_Field_TransformFileName_Help
data.value: Supported tokens: {DATE:Format} - Inserts file modification date in the specified format. To see all options google "C# DateTime format".

data.name: ImportMediaTask_Help
data.value:
Imports audios, photos, videos from SD Card of camera, recorder; photos and videos from your phone via WI-FI through FTP Server application using template file names.
See application web-site for documentation how to setup file transmission via WI-FI through FTP Server application on the phone.

data.name: ImportMediaTask_DeleteCopiedDataOnSourceMedia
data.value: Delete copied data on source media

data.name: ImproveIt_Message
data.value:
An unexpected error occurred.
Please contact the developers with information saved on your desktop in "{0}"
Application will now close.

data.name: IncrementalBackup_Help
data.value:
The most basic form of incremental backup consists of identifying, recording and thus, preserving only those files that have changed since the last backup.
Since changes are typically low, incremental backups are much smaller and quicker than full backups.
For instance, following a full backup on Friday, a Monday backup will contain only those files that changed since Friday. A Tuesday backup contains only those files that changed since Monday, and so on.
Should any one of the copies created fail, including the first (full), restoration will be incomplete.

data.name: Installer_AdminRequired
data.value: Administrator privileges required to install for all users. Please run the installer as administrator or use /CURRENTUSER.

data.name: Installer_AdminRequiredDetailed
data.value:
Administrator privileges are required to install for all users.
Please either: - Choose 'Install for current user only', or - Run this installer as administrator

data.name: Installer_AllUsersDescription
data.value: The application will be available for all users on this computer.

data.name: Installer_ArchRequired
data.value: This installer requires a x64 or arm64 version of Windows.

data.name: Installer_CurrentUserDescription
data.value: Recommended. The application will only be available for your user account.

data.name: Installer_InstallModeDescription
data.value: Please choose whether you want to install BUtil for yourself only or for all users of this computer.

data.name: Installer_InstallModeSubtitle
data.value: Select who can use this application

data.name: LeftMenu_What_Hint
data.value: Here you may add folders and files you need to process.

data.name: LeftMenu_When_Help
data.value: Here you can set up a scheduler. Shceduler can help you to automate task launch.

data.name: LeftMenu_Where_Help_DragDrop
data.value: Drag and drop here files and folders from Explorer.

data.name: LeftMenu_Where_Hint
data.value: In this place you can add locations where you would like your files to be copied.

data.name: Name_Field_Validation
data.value: Name is empty or contains not supported path characters.

data.name: Name_Field_Validation_ExceedsLimit
data.value: Name '{0}' length exceeds limit by {1} chars.

data.name: Name_Field_Validation_Duplicate
data.value: A task with this name already exists.

data.name: Password_Help
data.value: Recommended minimum password length is 50 characters and more.

data.name: RestoreFrom_Field_Validation_NoStateFiles
data.value:
Cannot locate files "{0}" in directory.
Point to a directory containing this file.

data.name: Server_Field_Address_Validation
data.value: Server name is not be specified.

data.name: SFTP_Validation_PasswordAndKeyNotSpecified
data.value: Both password or key file are empty.

data.name: SFTPFolder_Field_Validation_InvalidPostfix
data.value: SFTP folder must not end on /.

data.name: SFTPFolder_Field_Validation_RootFolder
data.value: SFTP folder / is not allowed. Folder must point to more specific folder.

data.name: State_LoadFromEverywhere
data.value: Get state of source items and storages

data.name: StorageItem_ExcludePattern_Help
data.value: Here you can specify file globbing exclude patterns. It can be a file name or folder name or wildcard. Examples: d:\temp, d:\temp\file.txt, d:\temp\**\*.txt or bin. See detailed description in the following link.

data.name: StorageItem_ExcludePattern_Hint
data.value: Skips processing of files by exclude pattern.

data.name: SynchronizationTask_Help
data.value: Two-way synchronization of files between devices using centralized storage.

data.name: Task_Delete_Confirm
data.value: Do you really want to delete "{0}"?

data.name: Task_Edit_Validation_NoCLI
data.value: You cannot edit this type of task in CLI.

data.name: Task_Status_PartialDueToQuota
data.value: Completed partially. Due to storage quota some files were skipped this time. Remaining {0} files with of {1} GB.

data.name: Task_Validation_NotSupported
data.value: This task type is not supported.

data.name: TechnicalTool_Error_PathRequired
data.value: Specify both source and output file paths.

data.name: TrustDetected_Ftps_Instruction
data.value: Verify certificate identity out-of-band, then click Save again to store it.

data.name: TrustDetected_Ftps_Title
data.value: FTPS server certificate detected during test.

data.name: TrustDetected_Sftp_Instruction
data.value: Verify this fingerprint out-of-band, then click Save again to store it.

data.name: TrustDetected_Sftp_Title
data.value: SFTP server trust information detected during test.

data.name: UploadFolderTask_Help
data.value: You can upload folder contents with overwriting already existing files.

data.name: S3_Provider_AlibabaCloudOSS_Help
data.value:
Alibaba Cloud OSS — S3-compatible object storage from Alibaba.
1. In OSS Console (https://oss.console.aliyun.com/), create a bucket. Note its region (e.g. oss-cn-hangzhou). 2. In RAM Console → Users → create a user → attach AliyunOSSFullAccess → create an AccessKey. Copy the AccessKey ID (= Access Key) and AccessKey Secret (= Secret Key). 3. Service URL: https://oss-<REGION>.aliyuncs.com (e.g. https://oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com). 4. Region: e.g. oss-cn-hangzhou.

data.name: S3_Provider_AWSS3_Help
data.value:
AWS S3 — Amazon's original object storage.
1. Open the S3 Console (https://s3.console.aws.amazon.com/) and create a bucket. Note its region (e.g. us-east-1). 2. Open IAM → Users → create a user → attach the AmazonS3FullAccess policy. 3. Under Security credentials, create an Access key — copy the Key ID and Secret. 4. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key, and Region.

data.name: S3_Provider_BaiduCloudBOS_Help
data.value:
Baidu Cloud BOS — S3-compatible object storage from Baidu.
1. In BOS Console (https://console.bce.baidu.com/bos/), create a bucket. Note its region (e.g. bj, gz, su). 2. Go to Security → Access Keys — copy your Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. 3. Service URL: https://s3.<REGION>.bcebos.com (e.g. https://s3.bj.bcebos.com). 4. Region: e.g. bj.

data.name: S3_Provider_BackblazeB2_Help
data.value:
Backblaze B2 — affordable S3-compatible storage.
1. In Backblaze (https://www.backblaze.com/) → B2 Cloud Storage → Buckets, create a bucket. 2. Go to App Keys → Add a New Application Key, scope it to the bucket. Copy the keyID (= Access Key) and applicationKey (= Secret Key). 3. Service URL: your bucket's endpoint shown in bucket details, e.g. https://s3.us-west-004.backblazeb2.com 4. Region: the segment between 's3.' and '.backblazeb2.com', e.g. us-west-004.

data.name: S3_Provider_CloudflareR2_Help
data.value:
Cloudflare R2 — zero-egress S3-compatible storage.
1. In Cloudflare Dashboard (https://dash.cloudflare.com/) → R2 → create a bucket. 2. Go to R2 → Manage R2 API Tokens → Create API Token with Object Read & Write. Copy the Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. 3. Service URL: https://<ACCOUNT_ID>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com Your Account ID is shown on the R2 overview page. 4. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key, and Service URL. Region is set to 'auto' automatically.

data.name: S3_Provider_CloudRuStorage_Help
data.value:
Cloud.ru (SberCloud) Object Storage — S3-compatible OBS storage.
1. In Cloud.ru Console (https://cloud.ru/) → Object Storage Service (OBS) → create a bucket. 2. Go to My Credentials → Access Keys → Add Access Key. Copy the Access Key and Secret Access Key. 3. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key, and Region (e.g. ru-moscow-1). Service URL is filled in automatically.

data.name: S3_Provider_Custom_Help
data.value:
MinIO or any S3-compatible server.
1. Start your MinIO (or other S3-compatible) server. 2. Create a bucket via the MinIO Console or 'mc mb <alias>/<bucket>'. 3. Create an access key in MinIO Console → Access Keys (avoid root credentials in production). 4. Service URL: your server endpoint, e.g. http://192.168.1.10:9000 or https://minio.example.com. 5. Region: any value (e.g. us-east-1) — or leave empty if the server does not enforce it.

data.name: S3_Provider_DigitalOceanSpaces_Help
data.value:
DigitalOcean Spaces — S3-compatible object storage.
1. In Spaces (https://cloud.digitalocean.com/spaces), create a Space. Note its region (e.g. nyc3). 2. Go to API → Spaces Keys → Generate New Key. Copy the Key (= Access Key) and Secret. 3. Service URL: https://<REGION>.digitaloceanspaces.com (e.g. https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com). 4. Region: same short code, e.g. nyc3.

data.name: S3_Provider_GoogleCloudStorage_Help
data.value:
Google Cloud Storage — via the S3 interoperability API.
1. In Google Cloud Console (https://console.cloud.google.com/storage), create a bucket. 2. Go to Cloud Storage → Settings → Interoperability → create an HMAC key for a service account. Copy the Access Key and Secret. 3. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key (HMAC key), Secret Key (HMAC secret). Service URL and region are set automatically.

data.name: S3_Provider_HuaweiCloudOBS_Help
data.value:
Huawei Cloud OBS — S3-compatible object storage from Huawei.
1. In OBS Console (https://console.huaweicloud.com/obs/), create a bucket. Note its region (e.g. cn-north-4). 2. Go to My Credentials → Access Keys → Create Access Key. Copy the AK (= Access Key) and SK (= Secret Key). 3. Service URL: https://obs.<REGION>.myhuaweicloud.com (e.g. https://obs.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com). 4. Region: e.g. cn-north-4.

data.name: S3_Provider_LinodeObjectStorage_Help
data.value:
Linode (Akamai) Object Storage — S3-compatible storage.
1. In Linode Cloud Manager (https://cloud.linode.com/object-storage/buckets), create a bucket. Note its cluster. 2. Go to Object Storage → Access Keys → Create an Access Key scoped to the bucket. Copy the Access Key and Secret Key. 3. Service URL: https://<CLUSTER>.linodeobjects.com (e.g. https://us-east-1.linodeobjects.com). 4. Region: same cluster code, e.g. us-east-1.

data.name: S3_Provider_QiniuCloudKodo_Help
data.value:
Qiniu Cloud Kodo — S3-compatible storage from Qiniu.
1. In Qiniu Portal (https://portal.qiniu.com/kodo/bucket), create a bucket. Note its region. 2. Go to Personal Center → Key Management — copy your AccessKey and SecretKey. 3. Service URL by region: East China: https://s3-cn-east-1.qiniucs.com North China: https://s3-cn-north-1.qiniucs.com South China: https://s3-cn-south-1.qiniucs.com North America: https://s3-us-north-1.qiniucs.com 4. Region: matching code, e.g. cn-east-1.

data.name: S3_Provider_ScalewayObjectStorage_Help
data.value:
Scaleway Object Storage — European S3-compatible storage.
1. In Scaleway Console (https://console.scaleway.com/object-storage/buckets), create a bucket. Note its region (e.g. fr-par). 2. Go to IAM → API Keys → Generate API Key with Object Storage write permissions. Copy the Access Key and Secret Key. 3. Service URL: https://s3.<REGION>.scw.cloud (e.g. https://s3.fr-par.scw.cloud). 4. Region: e.g. fr-par, nl-ams, pl-waw.

data.name: S3_Provider_SelectelStorage_Help
data.value:
Selectel Object Storage — S3-compatible Russian storage.
1. In Selectel Panel (https://my.selectel.ru/) → Cloud Storage → create a container (= bucket). 2. Under Users & Roles, create a service user with storage access → S3 Keys → create a key pair. Copy the Access Key and Secret Key. 3. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key. Service URL is filled in automatically.

data.name: S3_Provider_TencentCloudCOS_Help
data.value:
Tencent Cloud COS — S3-compatible object storage from Tencent.
1. In COS Console (https://console.cloud.tencent.com/cos), create a bucket. Note the AppId and Region (e.g. ap-beijing). 2. In Access Management → API Keys → Create Key. Copy the SecretId (= Access Key) and SecretKey (= Secret Key). 3. Service URL: https://cos.<REGION>.myqcloud.com (e.g. https://cos.ap-beijing.myqcloud.com). 4. Bucket name format: <name>-<AppId> (e.g. mybucket-1250000000). Region: e.g. ap-beijing.

data.name: S3_Provider_VKCloudStorage_Help
data.value:
VK Cloud Object Storage — S3-compatible storage from VK Cloud.
1. In VK Cloud Portal (https://msk.cloud.vk.com/) → Cloud Storage → create a bucket. 2. Under your account → Access Keys → create a new key pair. Copy the Access Key and Secret Key. 3. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key, and Region if required (e.g. ru-msk). Service URL is filled in automatically.

data.name: S3_Provider_VolcanoEngineTOS_Help
data.value:
Volcano Engine TOS (ByteDance) — S3-compatible storage.
1. In Volcano Engine Console (https://console.volcengine.com/tos), create a bucket. Note its region (e.g. cn-beijing). 2. Go to IAM → Access Keys → create a key. Copy the Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. 3. Service URL: https://tos-s3-<REGION>.volces.com (e.g. https://tos-s3-cn-beijing.volces.com). 4. Region: e.g. cn-beijing.

data.name: S3_Provider_Wasabi_Help
data.value:
Wasabi — hot cloud storage with no egress fees.
1. In Wasabi Console (https://console.wasabisys.com/) → Buckets → Create Bucket. Choose a region. 2. Go to Access Keys → Create New Access Key. Copy the Access Key and Secret Key. 3. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key, and Region (e.g. us-east-1, eu-central-1). Service URL is filled in automatically.

data.name: S3_Provider_YandexObjectStorage_Help
data.value:
Yandex Object Storage — S3-compatible Russian cloud storage.
1. In Yandex Cloud Console (https://console.yandex.cloud/) → Object Storage → create a bucket. 2. Go to Service Accounts → create one with the storage.editor role → create a Static access key. Copy the Key ID (= Access Key) and Secret key. 3. Fill in: Bucket, Access Key, Secret Key. Service URL and region are set automatically.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_Box_Help
data.value:
Box — enterprise cloud storage with WebDAV support.
1. Log in at app.box.com. Note that Box WebDAV is available only for paid plans. 2. Enter your Box account email as the User. 3. Enter your Box password as the Password. If you use SSO, you may need to set a separate WebDAV password in Account Settings. Server address, HTTPS, port, and base path are configured automatically.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_Custom_Help
data.value:
Generic WebDAV server (Nextcloud, ownCloud, Synology DSM, Seafile, Apache, etc.)
1. Enter the server Address, choose HTTPS (recommended), and set the port (0 = default: 80 for HTTP, 443 for HTTPS). 2. Enter the base path for the WebDAV endpoint. Nextcloud example: /remote.php/dav/files/<username>/Backups Synology DSM: / 3. Enter your username and password (or an app-specific password if your server requires it).

data.name: WebDav_Preset_IONOSHiDrive_Help
data.value:
IONOS HiDrive — German cloud storage with strong privacy focus.
1. Log in at hidrive.ionos.com → Settings → Apps & connected services → WebDAV. Or access WebDAV with your existing credentials directly. 2. Enter your HiDrive username as the User. 3. Enter your HiDrive password as the Password. Server address, HTTPS, port, and base path are configured automatically.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_Jianguoyun_Help
data.value:
Jianguoyun (坚果云) — popular Chinese cloud storage with native WebDAV support.
1. Log in at jianguoyun.com → Account Info → Security → Third-Party App Management → Add Application. Copy the generated app password — it is shown only once. 2. Enter your Jianguoyun email address as the User. 3. Enter the generated app password as the Password. Server address, HTTPS, port, and base path are configured automatically.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_Koofr_Help
data.value:
Koofr — privacy-friendly cloud storage based in Slovenia (EU).
1. Go to app.koofr.eu → Preferences → Password → App passwords → Add password. 2. Enter your Koofr account email as the User. 3. Enter the generated app password as the Password. Server address, HTTPS, port, and base path are configured automatically.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_MailRuCloud_Help
data.value:
Mail.ru Cloud via WebDAV.
1. Go to account.mail.ru → Security → App passwords → Create app password. Select 'Mail' or 'All' as the type, then copy the generated password. 2. Enter your Mail.ru email address as the User. 3. Enter the app password as the Password. Server address, HTTPS, port, and base path are configured automatically.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_Nextcloud_Help
data.value:
Nextcloud — popular self-hosted file sync and share.
1. Enter your Nextcloud server address (e.g. nextcloud.example.com). 2. The base path is pre-filled: /remote.php/dav/files/<username>/ Replace <username> with your actual Nextcloud username. 3. Enter your Nextcloud username and password (or an app password from Settings → Security → Devices & sessions → App passwords).

data.name: WebDav_Preset_ownCloud_Help
data.value:
ownCloud — self-hosted file sync and share.
1. Enter your ownCloud server address (e.g. owncloud.example.com). 2. The base path is pre-filled: /remote.php/dav/files/<username>/ Replace <username> with your actual ownCloud username. 3. Enter your ownCloud username and password (or an app password if 2FA is enabled).

data.name: WebDav_Preset_pCloud_Help
data.value:
pCloud — cloud storage with EU and US data centers.
1. Log in at my.pcloud.com → Settings → Security → App Passwords → Create. Copy the app password — it is shown only once. 2. Enter your pCloud account email as the User. 3. Enter the app password as the Password. For EU data center: ewebdav.pcloud.com is used automatically. For US data center: change the host to webdav.pcloud.com.

data.name: WebDav_Preset_YandexDisk_Help
data.value:
Yandex Disk via WebDAV.
1. Go to id.yandex.com → Security → App passwords → Create app password for 'WebDAV access'. Copy the generated password — it is shown only once. 2. Enter your Yandex login as the User. 3. Enter the app password (not your Yandex account password) as the Password. Server address, HTTPS, and port are configured automatically.

data.name: Winget_Description
Winget_ShortDescription
data.value: BUtil creates incremental backups, synchronization, and media imports on Windows and Linux with deduplication and support for WebDAV, S3-compatible, NFS, Azure Blob, SFTP, FTPS, and SMB/CIFS storages.
